step.title: Prepare Prototype Location
step.goal: Create the prototype directory inside the bundle. For UI prototypes, detect the project's design system and load its conventions so the scaffold in step 3 uses them.

substep.title: Create Prototype Directory
substep.action:
Verify the directory exists. Store as {{session.prototype_path}}.
Add a `.gitkeep` or note file inside if the convention requires it (some repos require non-empty directories for git tracking). The prototype directory IS tracked by git per spec §4.2 — bundles live in shared/ which is committed.

check.action: Proceed to substep 2c (LOGIC-specific location prep is minimal).
Proceed to substep 2d for design-system detection.

substep.title: LOGIC Location Prep
substep.action:
Detect the language ambient in the codebase from prior orientation (or from a quick re-check of the repo top-level config files): - package.json present -> TypeScript or JavaScript - pyproject.toml or requirements.txt present -> Python (prefer with PEP 723 inline metadata, runnable via `uv run`) - Cargo.toml present -> Rust - go.mod present -> Go
Pick ONE language for the prototype. Default to whatever is dominant. Store as {{session.language}}.
For a Python prototype with external deps, use PEP 723 inline metadata (matches the convention in .vera/shared/scripts/). For a Node/TypeScript prototype, prefer ESM single-file runnable via `bun` or `node --experimental-strip-types`.

substep.title: UI Design-System Detection
substep.action:
Detect a design system in the project. Check (in order):
1. **Component library imports**: read package.json or pyproject.toml. Common signals: - @shadcn/ui, shadcn-ui -> shadcn/ui (Tailwind + Radix) - @mantine/core -> Mantine - @radix-ui/* -> Radix primitives - @mui/material, @material-ui/core -> Material UI - antd -> Ant Design - chakra-ui -> Chakra UI - tailwindcss -> Tailwind (style system, not component library)
2. **Config files**: presence of - tailwind.config.js / tailwind.config.ts -> Tailwind - styled-components -> CSS-in-JS - sass/scss directories -> Sass conventions
3. **Internal design system**: look for - src/ui/, src/components/, src/design-system/, packages/ui/ - A README or index.ts in those directories that documents primitives like Button, Card, Input
4. **None detected**: proceed with vanilla HTML + minimal CSS (use system fonts and a neutral palette; do NOT invent a brand look)
Store as {{session.design_system}}: { kind, source_path or url, key_primitives }.

check.action:
Read 1-2 representative component files from the project's design system or its docs. Capture: - The primary primitives in use (Button, Card, Input, etc.) - The styling approach (Tailwind classes? CSS variables? CSS-in-JS?) - The naming/casing convention for class names
The prototype in step 3 will mimic these to produce evidence that looks like the real product would look.

substep.title: Display Location Summary

section.title: Design system fidelity matters
section.description: A UI prototype using arbitrary styling shows what something COULD look like, not what it WOULD look like in the actual product. That produces misleading evidence — the user might approve a prototype that, when built with the real design system, looks completely different. For UI prototypes the design system check is a substep, not a suggestion.

rules.rule: If the project has shadcn/ui, the prototype uses shadcn/ui classes (or a CDN-loaded equivalent)
If the project has Tailwind, the prototype uses Tailwind utility classes
If the project has an internal design system, the prototype mimics its component naming and styling approach
"None detected" is fine; use neutral defaults. Do not invent a brand look

section.title: Bundle directory containment
section.description:
Prototypes live INSIDE the bundle directory at `prototypes/{name}/`. This co-locates evidence with the ticket that produced it. Thoughts CLI tracks shared/ but does not validate non-`.md` files; the prototype subdirectory is invisible to validation.
When the bundle is archived (status: archived), the prototype directory archives with it. This matches the throwaway discipline: prototypes are evidence for the ticket lifecycle, not durable artifacts.

section.title: When the bundle directory doesn't exist
section.description: This skill assumes the caller (ticket-grill) is operating on a bundle scaffolded by ticket-orient. If the bundle directory does not exist, halt and surface the error — the prototype skill is not the place to scaffold bundles.
